msg: Given a task parameter is set by the client, if a set of tasks yields 25% of attempts failed due to no connectivity...isnt that a waste of their time and effort? It makes total sense for them to say that they want their tests run on phones in the upper percentiles of connectivity.

author: Matt
msg:
At the same time, not every region will have a test running all the time till things build out more. So, people with high uptime can still have little demand for their area. 
It is still early. How many telecoms are there in the world?

author: Matt
msg: Definitely going to be a slow build to consistent numbers. Wow potential though.

author: Matt
msg: Stated 20 customers.  Not too bad 6 months in on a new concept yet to be proven. Imagine a tenx on customer numbers and a 10x on task types offered! I dont know about every area but in my area I could pic from probably 10 carriers without searching at all. We've barely even started.

author: Wattdog
msg: Not listened to the ama yet and tried to read certain bits on here, what’s going on with the CLI atm, I’ve had 1 call. It’s customer based I know but just not interested in my area or something?

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
**13:29** Okay, so moving forward, we have the CLI test network. Everything looks fantastic. We've got a couple of large customers online. We are looking forward to having more customers online. We do have quite a lot of exposure to different customers and potential due to obviously our position in the telecommunications market. You know, we're able to get in contact with these types of people in need of these services.
...
**19:49** We're talking to some very renowned names in the industry, some very large names. They do appreciate the network that's been built. They appreciate the growth pattern. They understand where we're culling users, etc. And they think it's a tremendous win, right? There is obviously the exchange that's being rolled out right now. That exchange is, you know, not only a first of its kind in the industry, but it will also require CLI test volumes and SMS integrity, message integrity detection and to ensure that sender IDs are sent correctly, right? So it has a lot of potential for growth, an enormous amount of potential for growth. We're seeing quite a lot of calls every day to the network and we're sort of happy with the way it's being perceived and received by customers.
...
**28:04** Actually, so how it works for CLI is we actually had an in-house sort of staging environment that was used to set up these tests at scale. A simulation of end-to-end CLI testing took place and now CLI testing is live on the production network and customers are happy. I have noted that some users are receiving a notification but not a call and some users have received a call but not a notification. That issue has been solved internally and I believe that it was pushed yesterday.
...
**45:57** Well then I think this final question from Chile: are the signed contracts you've talked about paid orders with committed volume or spend or agreements to try it or plans to work together?
- There's a variety of contracts. Most of them are pay as you go. There are some long-standing agreements that are in place, but a lot of customers use the network and pay for what they use. We're going to be promoting that model going forward. We think it's a very flexible model. Whilst we do have agreements with third parties, such as Apex from Scouts and Runner, and some of the newer tasks, as well as the SMS and CLI tasks have long-standing contracts in place, but the actual usage generally is pay for usage.
...
**1:14:22** No CLI calls for me yet. Got an email from Vodafone a few days ago. They have implemented scam call protection. Just wondering if this would impact the ability for me to receive CLI calls.
- No, absolutely not. The CLI verification calls that come through are not spam calls whatsoever. They are initiated by an operator who wishes to determine the status or quality of the CLI delivery on his voice route or SMS route for that note. These are not qualified as spam calls. You have opted in to receive them in the task list and they should not be flagged or blocked by any call protection system. Quite the opposite, they are ensuring that the customers' calls are protected by qualifying the CLI
 delivery capabilities of a given route at any given time.
...
https://x.com/i/spaces/1yxBeMlaXMLJN
